steps.title: Create a one-year growth table
steps.content: The model starts at 15,000 people, so use $(0,15000)$. A 4% increase means the next year's population is $15000(1.04)=15600$, so use $(1,15600)$. Fit an exponential model in Desmos.

steps.title: Read the model
steps.content: The regression gives $a=15000$ and $b=1.04$. In $y=a b^x$, that means the model is $$p=15000(1.04)^x.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D has the correct initial value 15,000 and growth factor 1.04. Choices A and C use 0.96, which models a decrease, and choice B puts 15,000 in the base instead of the coefficient. Choice D is correct.
